delivery: SERVER-SIDE ONLY. Answer indices must never be sent to the client. The server selects, shuffles, and grades. One bank per unit id. Not MDM-exclusive: F1-F5 (Foundation domain) share this same map.

objectives.whatisfrc: What FRC is and what a match looks like.
objectives.roles: The many roles on a team.

items.stem: What is FRC?
options: A contest where high school teams build a robot to play a game

items.stem: About how long is one match?
How many teams join together as one alliance?
What is autonomous?
options: The part where the robot drives itself using code
The part where a driver controls the robot

items.stem: What happens at Kickoff?
About how long is build season?
Who does the work on an FRC team?
options: Many people in many roles, like design, build, code, strategy, and scouting

items.stem: What is the correct order of the four steps in a unit?
What score do you need to pass a quiz?
How fast do you move through the track?
options: Everyone must finish on the same day

items.stem: What is the Drill step for?
options: To practice and get ready before the quiz
To prove you already learned it

items.stem: Do you need experience before you start this track?
options: No, everyone starts as a beginner
Yes, you must already drive the robot
Yes, you must know how to weld

items.stem: What happens at Kickoff?
What must the team do before building anything?
options: Understand the game and its rules

items.stem: About how long is build season?
Which best describes build season?
options: Design, build, test, and fix, over and over
Building it perfectly the first time

items.stem: What do qualification matches decide?
What happens in alliance selection?
What are the playoffs?
options: Elimination rounds that decide the winner

items.stem: What is the off-season for?
options: Learning skills, training new members, and preparing

items.stem: What can teams that do well move on to?
The season is best described as what?
options: A sprint where everyone has a part to play

items.stem: What comes first in the shop, always?
When do you wear safety glasses?
options: Any time tools are running, even when watching

items.stem: You have long hair and loose sleeves near a spinning machine. What do you do?
options: Tie back hair and remove loose sleeves

items.stem: You have never used a certain machine. What should you do?
options: Get trained or ask a mentor before using it
Use it while no one is looking

items.stem: You are not sure how a tool works. What is the right move?
You get a small cut. What do you do?
You find a broken tool. What do you do?
Why clean up messes on the floor?
options: So no one trips or slips

items.stem: On the most dangerous machines, you should...
Is a robot ever worth an injury?
The design process is best described as what?
options: A straight line with one pass

items.stem: What do you do first?
Why come up with several ideas instead of one?
options: A better chance of finding a good one

items.stem: What is a quick, rough first version for?
options: To learn whether the idea works

items.stem: What does testing do?
options: Shows what works and what to fix

items.stem: A test failed. What is the healthy response?
options: Learn what to fix and try again

items.stem: "The first version is never the last" means what?
Does this process only work for robot parts?
options: No, it works for any problem

items.stem: What happens after you improve a version?
options: You test it again and repeat the loop

items.stem: If you skip understanding the problem, what can happen?
options: You build the wrong thing well

items.stem: What is the engineering notebook?
options: The team's written record of what it did, why, and what it learned

items.stem: Why does the notebook matter?
options: New members can pick up work and the team avoids repeating mistakes
It is required to be long

items.stem: What should an entry include?
options: What you did, why, results, the date, and your name

items.stem: Should you record failures?
options: Yes, the team learns from them

items.stem: Who contributes to the notebook?
options: Everyone who works on the robot

items.stem: What is a good test of an entry?
options: Could someone else continue your work from it

items.stem: Why write down why you chose something?
options: So the team remembers the reason and does not re-argue it

items.stem: What does hiding a failure lead to?
Judges use the notebook to do what?
Notebook entries should be what?

objectives.views: Read orthographic views and their layout.
objectives.titleblock: Know what the title block carries.

items.stem: A circle in the top view and a pair of dashed lines in the front view represent what?
A hole callout of 0.266 diameter THRU tells a machinist to make what?
options: A 0.266 inch hole all the way through

items.stem: A hole shows a size callout but no dimensions to any edge. What is wrong?
options: Its position is undefined and cannot be made correctly

items.stem: Where do you find the part's material and the default tolerance?
Dashed lines on a drawing represent what?
A thin long-short-long chain line through a hole marks what?
In third-angle projection, where is the top view placed?
options: To the left of the front view

items.stem: In third-angle projection, where is the right-side view placed?
options: To the right of the front view
To the left of the front view

items.stem: Why does a part need a 2D drawing when a 3D model exists?
options: To define the part unambiguously for manufacturing

items.stem: A dimension preceded by the diameter symbol specifies what?
A note reading R0.25 specifies what?
To confirm a hole is fully defined on a drawing, you check that it has what?
options: A size callout and locating dimensions

items.stem: How do features relate across aligned orthographic views?
options: They are drawn at random positions
They line up, so front and top share width and front and side share height
Each view uses a different scale

items.stem: The word THRU in a hole callout means what?
options: The hole goes completely through the material

items.stem: You want the stiffest way to hold two plates apart. What do you use, and why?
options: A spacer with a through-bolt, since the bolt is preloaded in tension
A threaded standoff, since threads add stiffness

items.stem: Which fastener family is the structural workhorse of FRC?
Why has the 10-32 fine thread become so common?
options: Most current COTS parts are threaded 10-32

items.stem: A joint on a pivot vibrates every match. Which nut resists loosening?
Two meshing 20 diametral pitch gears have 24 and 60 teeth. What is their center distance?
Which is a current high-performance FRC motor?
Which motor is obsolete and not used on new designs?
A gear locks to a shaft and turns with it. What shaft profile does that?
What surface does a bearing need to ride on?
A gear is described by which two properties?
Which vendor is the go-to for raw fasteners, bearings, and stock?
You must fasten to material too thin to tap. Which nut presses into the part?
Which is the current FRC radio?
Why standardize on a few screw sizes and one shaft standard?
options: Fewer spares and tools, and faster pit repairs
It is required by the rules

items.stem: You are fastening into a 1/2 inch aluminum block with no room for a nut. Which hole?
A clearance hole is sized how relative to the screw?
A tapped hole's drill is sized how relative to the screw?
What diameter is an 8-32 clearance hole?
What does the Hole Wizard do that a sketched circle does not?
options: Sizes the hole for the screw and carries the callout to the drawing
Adds threads to any part automatically

items.stem: A pivot vibrates every match. Which nut type belongs there?
A keps nut has what feature?
Rivets are used to join parts how?
options: Only with a nut on the back

items.stem: When is a 1/4-20 fastener used?
A callout of 0.203 diameter THRU describes what?
options: A 10-24 clearance hole through the part

items.stem: Which makes a stiffer, preloaded joint?
options: A short screw into thin material
A through-bolt with a spacer in compression

items.stem: Why keep enough material around a hole (edge distance)?
options: To prevent the edge from tearing out under load

items.stem: A pem nut is used when the material is what?
A clearance hole lets the screw do what?
options: Pass through freely to a nut or the far part's threads

MDM-10.title: Tolerancing and GD and T Basics

objectives.gdt: GD and T controls and datums.

items.stem: A tolerance is what?
options: The allowed variation on a dimension

items.stem: What happens to a dimension with no stated tolerance?
options: It falls back to the title-block default

items.stem: A bearing is pressed into a plate bore. Which fit is that?
A shaft slides and spins inside a bearing. Which fit is that?
For a laser-cut slot-and-tab joint, how do you adjust for a good fit?
options: Shrink the slot and grow the tab
Open the slot about 0.005 and shrink the tab about 0.005

items.stem: A feature control frame specifies what?
options: A geometric control, a tolerance zone, and datums

items.stem: Datums are what?
options: The reference features everything else is measured from

items.stem: Which of these are GD and T geometric controls?
Why not put a tight tolerance on every dimension?
options: Tight tolerances waste shop time, so tolerance only what needs it

items.stem: A transition fit is what?
options: Between a clearance and an interference fit
The same as a clearance fit

items.stem: A torque-carrying shaft section is often made slightly long by about how much, and why?
options: About 0.01 inch, to absorb part-width and manufacturing variation

items.stem: Where does tight tolerancing and GD and T belong in FRC?
options: On precision interfaces like bearing bores and bolt patterns

items.stem: GD and T controls what beyond size?
Why does a press fit stay put?
options: The part is slightly larger than the bore and is pressed in
